children.heading: Petition by persons adversely affected, consumers, or consumer organizations
children.label: a Petition by persons adversely affected, consumers, or consumer organizations
children.text: Not later than 60 days after a consumer product safety rule is promulgated by the Commission, any person adversely affected by such rule, or any consumer or consumer organization, may file a petition with the United States court of appeals for the District of Columbia, or for the circuit in which such person, consumer, or organization resides or has his principal place of business for judicial review of such rule. Copies of the petition shall be forthwith transmitted by the clerk of the court to the Commission or other officer designated by it for that purpose and to the Attorney General. The record of the proceedings on which the Commission based its rule shall be filed in the court as provided for in . For purposes of this section, the term “record” means such consumer product safety rule; any notice or proposal published pursuant to section 2056, 2057, or 2058 of this title; the transcript required by of any oral presentation; any written submission of interested parties; and any other information which the Commission considers relevant to such rule.section 2112 of title 28section 2058(d)(2) of this title

children.heading: Additional data, views, or arguments
children.label: b Additional data, views, or arguments
children.text: If the petitioner applies to the court for leave to adduce additional data, views, or arguments and shows to the satisfaction of the court that such additional data, views, or arguments are material and that there were reasonable grounds for the petitioner’s failure to adduce such data, views, or arguments in the proceeding before the Commission, the court may order the Commission to provide additional opportunity for the oral presentation of data, views, or arguments and for written submissions. The Commission may modify its findings, or make new findings by reason of the additional data, views, or arguments so taken and shall file such modified or new findings, and its recommendation, if any, for the modification or setting aside of its original rule, with the return of such additional data, views, or arguments.

children.heading: Jurisdiction; costs and attorneys’ fees; substantial evidence to support administrative findings
children.label: c Jurisdiction; costs and attorneys’ fees; substantial evidence to support administrative findings
children.text: Upon the filing of the petition under subsection (a) of this section the court shall have jurisdiction to review the consumer product safety rule in accordance with chapter 7 of title 5, and to grant appropriate relief, including interim relief, as provided in such chapter. A court may in the interest of justice include in such relief an award of the costs of suit, including reasonable attorneys’ fees (determined in accordance with subsection (f) and reasonable expert witnesses’ fees. Attorneys’ fees may be awarded against the United States (or any agency or official of the United States) without regard to or any other provision of law. The consumer product safety rule shall not be affirmed unless the Commission’s findings under sections 2058(f)(1) and 2058(f)(3) of this title are supported by substantial evidence on the record taken as a whole.1section 2412 of title 28So in original. Probably should be followed by a closing parenthesis.1

children.heading: Supreme Court review
children.label: d Supreme Court review
children.text: The judgment of the court affirming or setting aside, in whole or in part, any consumer product safety rule shall be final, subject to review by the Supreme Court of the United States upon certiorari or certification, as provided in .section 1254 of title 28

children.heading: Other remedies
children.label: e Other remedies
children.text: The remedies provided for in this section shall be in addition to and not in lieu of any other remedies provided by law.

children.heading: Computation of reasonable fee for attorney
children.label: f Computation of reasonable fee for attorney
children.text: For purposes of this section and sections 2072(a) and 2073 of this title, a reasonable attorney’s fee is a fee (1) which is based upon (A) the actual time expended by an attorney in providing advice and other legal services in connection with representing a person in an action brought under this section, and (B) such reasonable expenses as may be incurred by the attorney in the provision of such services, and (2) which is computed at the rate prevailing for the provision of similar services with respect to actions brought in the court which is awarding such fee.

children.label: A
children.text: any consumer product safety rule promulgated by the Commission pursuant to (relating to identification of substantial hazards);section 2064(j) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: any consumer product safety standard promulgated by the Commission pursuant to (relating to all-terrain vehicles);section 2089 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: any standard promulgated by the Commission under (relating to durable infant and toddler products); andsection 2056a of this title

children.label: D
children.text: any consumer product safety standard promulgated by the Commission under (relating to mandatory toy safety standards).section 2056b of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 2 In general
children.text: Not later than 60 days after the promulgation, by the Commission, of a rule or standard to which this subsection applies, any person adversely affected by such rule or standard may file a petition with the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit for judicial review of such rule. Copies of the petition shall be forthwith transmitted by the clerk of the court to the Commission or other officer designated by it for that purpose and to the Attorney General. The record of the proceedings on which the Commission based its rule shall be filed in the court as provided for in .section 2112 of title 28

children.heading: Review
children.label: 3 Review
children.text: Upon the filing of the petition under paragraph (2) of this subsection, the court shall have jurisdiction to review the rule in accordance with chapter 7 of title 5 and to grant appropriate relief, including interim relief, as provided in such chapter.

children.heading: Conclusiveness of judgment
children.label: 4 Conclusiveness of judgment
children.text: The judgment of the court affirming or setting aside, in whole or in part, any final rule under this section shall be final, subject to review by the Supreme Court of the United States upon certiorari or certification, as provided in .section 1254 of title 28

children.heading: Further review
children.label: 5 Further review
children.text: A rule or standard with respect to which this subsection applies shall not be subject to judicial review in proceedings under (relating to imported products) or in civil or criminal proceedings for enforcement.section 2066 of this title
